MLflow and MLflow Model Registry gave teams a unified way to track, version, and govern all models—batch and real-time. Unity Catalog enforced fine-grained access control and data lineage. Databricks Agent Bricks is now powering SnackGPT, a domain-specific generative AI assistant for brand and category teams to ask natural language questions like “What were our top-selling products last year?” or “Why did sales drop in Q1?” using real syndicated point-of-sales data.
...
Across sales execution, SKU recommendation models evaluate store potential using geography, demographics, and layout to recommend smarter product placement—delivering a 2–4% increase in store-level topline sales and improving sales rep effectiveness to around 80%. Demand forecasting models now support supply chain planning from six weeks to two years out, reducing finished goods inventory waste by 2–3% and improving forecast accuracy by 3–5%. Revenue growth management models run simulations before any price change, modeling whether customers would switch brands, change pack sizes, or reduce volume. “If a business was able to sell one additional product because of a model recommendation, we can directly measure that uplift,” Kulshrestha said. “It’s a revenue growth story.”
...
Today, Mondelez manages close to 20,000 models on Databricks, with around 3,000 in production—giving leadership a clear view of which models are production-grade, experimental, or retired. Technical debt from managing disparate systems has dropped significantly, and Delta Sharing is in roadmap to make reusable segmentation models available across regional teams. “Databricks has been so easy for the team to operate, empowering us to meet the needs of the business,” Kulshrestha concluded.

results.title: Next Generation Consumer Science Sprint - TechConnect
highlights:
Mondelez International, a global leader in fast moving consumer goods, seeks better insights into consumer sentiment through the usage of next-generation methodologies and technologies. Mondelez International intends to conduct a pilot-scale evaluation later this year.
...
All consumer goods companies rely on understanding the needs and wants of customers. Mondelez International already utilizes surveys, chatbots, in-person evaluation panels, and more to glean information about consumer sentiment regarding products and packaging. They believe that innovative methodologies and technologies are now able to deliver new and better insights into the consumer.
...
Mondelez International is interested in all methodologies and technologies that offer significant improvements over conventional alternatives specifically applied to qualitative research, including approaches from fields outside traditional qualitative research in consumer science, such as semiotics and anthropology.
...
Approaches of interest include, but are not limited to:
...
- CONNECT
- Methods adapted from other disciplines or applications, including innovative approaches for qualitative research moderation
- Methods incorporating new technologies or existing ones in innovative ways
- Integration of disparate data sets via AI platforms
- AI-enabled analysis of large consumer data sets
- Novel sensors
- Micro-expression detection systems
- New methods that complement traditional qualitative research
...
Kindly exclude new methods that are related to AI-based qualitative moderation (Mondelez International prefers humans to do that) and anything to do with synthetic consumers/digital consumers, etc.
...
Responses proposing methodologies or technologies validated through prior field deployment or consumer testing are of greatest interest.
...
Mondelez International intends to pilot one proposed methodology or technology during Q3 2026. Consequently, responses unable to meet that timetable are unlikely to be of interest. Mondelez International and the selected respondent will jointly determine parameters of the pilot test (i.e. location, scope, audience, etc.).
